title: ONGC Geology — Advanced Mock 12 — Conceptual
pattern: ONGC CBT — 85 MCQ · 120 min · 1 mark each · no negative marking

questions.subject: Domain Knowledge
questions.stem: In a plutonic system with 4 components and 4 phases under isobaric conditions, the variance according to the modified Gibbs phase rule is:
questions.solution: Isobaric modified phase rule: F = C - P + 1. With C=4, P=4: F = 1. Univariant cotectic line.

questions.subject: Domain Knowledge
questions.stem: A rock subjected to sigma1=100 MPa, sigma2=60 MPa, sigma3=20 MPa. The maximum shear stress is:
questions.solution: tau_max = (sigma1 - sigma3)/2 = 40 MPa. Intermediate stress sigma2 does not affect tau_max.

figure.markup: <svg xmlns="http://www.w3.org/2000/svg" viewBox="0 0 520 300"><rect width="520" height="300" fill="#fff" rx="8"/><text x="260" y="25" font-family="Arial" font-size="13" font-weight="bold" text-anchor="middle">3D Mohr Circle</text><line x1="100" y1="160" x2="420" y2="160" stroke="#333" stroke-width="1.5"/><line x1="260" y1="30" x2="260" y2="280" stroke="#333" stroke-width="1"/><circle cx="260" cy="160" r="100" fill="#e3f2fd" stroke="#1565c0" stroke-width="2"/><circle cx="160" cy="160" r="40" fill="#fce4ec" stroke="#c62828" stroke-width="2"/><circle cx="340" cy="160" r="20" fill="#fff3e0" stroke="#e65100" stroke-width="2"/><text x="130" y="180" font-family="Arial" font-size="10" fill="#c62828" font-weight="bold">sigma3=20</text><text x="330" y="180" font-family="Arial" font-size="10" fill="#e65100" font-weight="bold">sigma2=60</text><text x="400" y="180" font-family="Arial" font-size="10" fill="#1565c0" font-weight="bold">sigma1=100</text><text x="260" y="55" font-family="Arial" font-size="10" text-anchor="middle">tau_max = 40 MPa</text></svg>

questions.subject: Domain Knowledge
questions.stem: A mineral grain shows 1st-order grey birefringence (delta=0.009) at 30 um. At 60 um, the interference colour shifts to:
questions.solution: R = delta x t. At 30um: R=0.27um (1st-order grey). At 60um: R=0.54um (upper 1st-order, near 2nd-order boundary). Doubling thickness doubles retardation.

figure.markup: <svg xmlns="http://www.w3.org/2000/svg" viewBox="0 0 420 280"><rect width="420" height="280" fill="#fff" rx="8"/><text x="210" y="25" font-family="Arial" font-size="13" font-weight="bold" text-anchor="middle">Michel-Levy Chart (simplified)</text><rect x="40" y="40" width="340" height="180" fill="#fafafa" rx="4"/><text x="210" y="60" font-family="Arial" font-size="10" text-anchor="middle" fill="#333">Retardation (um)</text><rect x="60" y="70" width="80" height="30" fill="#bbb" rx="2"/><text x="100" y="90" font-family="Arial" font-size="9" text-anchor="middle" fill="#fff">1st-order grey</text><rect x="150" y="70" width="80" height="30" fill="#ffd54f" rx="2"/><text x="190" y="90" font-family="Arial" font-size="9" text-anchor="middle" fill="#333">1st-order yellow</text><rect x="240" y="70" width="80" height="30" fill="#90caf9" rx="2"/><text x="280" y="90" font-family="Arial" font-size="9" text-anchor="middle" fill="#333">2nd-order blue</text><text x="100" y="120" font-family="Arial" font-size="8" text-anchor="middle">0.27 um</text><text x="190" y="120" font-family="Arial" font-size="8" text-anchor="middle">0.54 um</text><line x1="100" y1="130" x2="100" y2="160" stroke="#f44336" stroke-width="2" stroke-dasharray="3,2"/><text x="100" y="175" font-family="Arial" font-size="8" text-anchor="middle" fill="#f44336">30um section</text><line x1="190" y1="130" x2="190" y2="160" stroke="#1565c0" stroke-width="2" stroke-dasharray="3,2"/><text x="190" y="175" font-family="Arial" font-size="8" text-anchor="middle" fill="#1565c0">60um section</text><text x="210" y="250" font-family="Arial" font-size="10" text-anchor="middle" fill="#333">Thickness doubling = colour order increase</text></svg>

questions.subject: Domain Knowledge
questions.stem: In a Bouma sequence, which division is most commonly missing when a turbidity current decelerates rapidly, bypassing the upper flow regime?
questions.solution: Division B (upper flow regime plane bed) is most commonly missing. Rapid deceleration skips from A directly to C. Common in proximal turbidites.

questions.subject: Domain Knowledge
questions.stem: During fractional crystallization of basaltic magma (olivine + cpx + plagioclase), which trace element is MOST incompatible?
questions.solution: LREE (La, Ce, Nd) are highly incompatible in all three phases (D<<1). Ni is compatible in olivine, Cr in cpx, Sr in plagioclase.

questions.subject: Domain Knowledge
questions.stem: If a taxon FAD is diachronous due to migration, which biozone concept is MOST affected?
questions.solution: Taxon-range zone defined by FAD+LAD of single taxon. Diachronous FAD makes boundaries diachronous. Assemblage zones use multiple taxa and are less sensitive.

questions.subject: Domain Knowledge
questions.stem: A pelitic schist contains garnet + staurolite + kyanite + muscovite. The kyanite presence indicates P-T conditions:
questions.solution: Kyanite is high-pressure polymorph. Staurolite = medium grade. Together: ~550-700C, 0.6-1.0 GPa, kyanite field.

figure.markup: <svg xmlns='http://www.w3.org/2000/svg' viewBox='0 0 440 340'><rect width='440' height='340' fill='#fff' rx='8'/><text x='220' y='25' font-family='Arial' font-size='13' font-weight='bold' text-anchor='middle'>Al2SiO5 Phase Diagram</text><line x1='70' y1='300' x2='400' y2='300' stroke='#333' stroke-width='1.5'/><line x1='70' y1='40' x2='70' y2='300' stroke='#333' stroke-width='1.5'/><text x='235' y='330' font-family='Arial' font-size='11' text-anchor='middle'>Temperature</text><text x='40' y='175' font-family='Arial' font-size='11' text-anchor='middle' transform='rotate(-90,40,175)'>Pressure</text><polygon points='190,85 310,255 110,255' fill='none' stroke='#999' stroke-width='1.5'/><text x='200' y='155' font-family='Arial' font-size='14' font-weight='bold' fill='#1565c0'>Kyanite</text><text x='105' y='240' font-family='Arial' font-size='12' fill='#2e7d32'>Andalusite</text><text x='290' y='240' font-family='Arial' font-size='12' fill='#c62828'>Sillimanite</text><circle cx='190' cy='85' r='5' fill='#ff6f00'/><text x='190' y='73' font-family='Arial' font-size='9' text-anchor='middle' fill='#ff6f00'>Triple Point</text></svg>

questions.subject: Domain Knowledge
questions.stem: Threshold capillary pressure for oil to enter a water-saturated carrier bed is inversely proportional to:
questions.solution: Pc = 2*gamma*cos(theta)/r. Inversely proportional to pore throat radius.

figure.markup: <svg xmlns="http://www.w3.org/2000/svg" viewBox="0 0 440 280"><rect width="440" height="280" fill="#fff" rx="8"/><text x="220" y="25" font-family="Arial" font-size="13" font-weight="bold" text-anchor="middle">Capillary Entry Pressure</text><rect x="40" y="45" width="360" height="40" fill="#b3e5fc" rx="3"/><text x="220" y="70" font-family="Arial" font-size="10" text-anchor="middle" fill="#01579b">Water-saturated carrier bed</text><rect x="40" y="95" width="360" height="35" fill="#ffe0b2" rx="3"/><text x="220" y="117" font-family="Arial" font-size="10" text-anchor="middle" fill="#e65100">Oil trying to enter (buoyancy)</text><circle cx="140" cy="165" r="15" fill="#e3f2fd" stroke="#1565c0" stroke-width="1.5"/><text x="140" y="169" font-family="Arial" font-size="8" text-anchor="middle">large</text><circle cx="300" cy="165" r="8" fill="#fce4ec" stroke="#c62828" stroke-width="1.5"/><text x="300" y="169" font-family="Arial" font-size="7" text-anchor="middle">small</text><text x="140" y="200" font-family="Arial" font-size="9" text-anchor="middle">Low Pc entry</text><text x="300" y="200" font-family="Arial" font-size="9" text-anchor="middle">High Pc entry</text><text x="220" y="240" font-family="Arial" font-size="10" text-anchor="middle" fill="#333">Pc = 2*gamma*cos(theta)/r</text><text x="220" y="260" font-family="Arial" font-size="9" text-anchor="middle" fill="#555">Oil migrates through largest pores first</text></svg>

questions.subject: Domain Knowledge
questions.stem: A type-1 sequence boundary is distinguished from type-2 by:
questions.solution: Type-1: truncation of coastal onlap + erosion + basinward facies shift. Type-2: lacks significant erosion.

questions.subject: Domain Knowledge
questions.stem: Anomalous fading in OSL dating primarily affects which mineral?
questions.solution: Feldspar: quantum mechanical tunneling of trapped electrons. Quartz does not show significant fading.

figure.markup: <svg xmlns="http://www.w3.org/2000/svg" viewBox="0 0 440 260"><rect width="440" height="260" fill="#fff" rx="8"/><text x="220" y="25" font-family="Arial" font-size="13" font-weight="bold" text-anchor="middle">OSL Anomalous Fading</text><rect x="40" y="45" width="160" height="100" fill="#e8f5e9" rx="4"/><text x="120" y="65" font-family="Arial" font-size="10" font-weight="bold" text-anchor="middle">Quartz</text><text x="120" y="85" font-family="Arial" font-size="9" text-anchor="middle">Stable signal</text><text x="120" y="100" font-family="Arial" font-size="9" text-anchor="middle">No fading</text><text x="120" y="120" font-family="Arial" font-size="9" text-anchor="middle" fill="#2e7d32">Reliable ages</text><rect x="240" y="45" width="160" height="100" fill="#ffebee" rx="4"/><text x="320" y="65" font-family="Arial" font-size="10" font-weight="bold" text-anchor="middle">Feldspar</text><text x="320" y="85" font-family="Arial" font-size="9" text-anchor="middle">Tunneling recombination</text><text x="320" y="100" font-family="Arial" font-size="9" text-anchor="middle">Signal fades</text><text x="320" y="120" font-family="Arial" font-size="9" text-anchor="middle" fill="#c62828">Underestimates ages</text><text x="220" y="180" font-family="Arial" font-size="10" text-anchor="middle" fill="#333">Correction: measure g-value, apply Huntley-Lamothe</text></svg>

questions.subject: Domain Knowledge
questions.stem: In DePaolo AFC model, trace element plot curvature is controlled by r, which equals:
options: Ratio of mass assimilated to mass crystallized
questions.solution: r = Ma/Mc. r=0 = pure FC. Higher r = more curved trends on log-log plots.

questions.subject: Domain Knowledge
questions.stem: Wilson carbonate facies 7 (fusulinid facies) represents:
options: Open marine shelf with fusulinids, echinoderms, brachiopods
questions.solution: Open marine shelf, normal salinity, diverse shelly fauna. Between reef margin (6) and restricted shelf (8).

questions.subject: Domain Knowledge
questions.stem: The stringer zone beneath a VMS deposit is characterized by:
options: Disseminated pyrite + chalcopyrite in chlorite-sericite alteration
questions.solution: Stockwork zone: disseminated pyrite + chalcopyrite (Cu-dominant) in chlorite-sericite alteration.

figure.markup: <svg xmlns='http://www.w3.org/2000/svg' viewBox='0 0 440 380'><rect width='440' height='380' fill='#fff' rx='8'/><text x='220' y='25' font-family='Arial' font-size='13' font-weight='bold' text-anchor='middle'>VMS Deposit</text><rect x='40' y='40' width='360' height='25' fill='#b3e5fc' rx='3'/><text x='220' y='57' font-family='Arial' font-size='10' text-anchor='middle' fill='#01579b'>Seawater</text><rect x='40' y='70' width='360' height='35' fill='#ffe0b2' rx='3'/><text x='220' y='93' font-family='Arial' font-size='10' text-anchor='middle' fill='#e65100'>Massive Sulfide (Zn-Cu-Pb)</text><ellipse cx='220' cy='140' rx='70' ry='30' fill='#fff9c4' stroke='#f9a825' stroke-width='2'/><text x='220' y='144' font-family='Arial' font-size='9' text-anchor='middle' fill='#e65100'>Pyrite + Sphalerite + Chalcopyrite</text><line x1='175' y1='170' x2='160' y2='240' stroke='#795548' stroke-width='2'/><line x1='265' y1='170' x2='280' y2='240' stroke='#795548' stroke-width='2'/><ellipse cx='220' cy='265' rx='50' ry='35' fill='#c8e6c9' stroke='#4caf50' stroke-width='2'/><text x='220' y='260' font-family='Arial' font-size='9' text-anchor='middle' fill='#1b5e20'>Stringer Zone</text><text x='220' y='275' font-family='Arial' font-size='8' text-anchor='middle' fill='#1b5e20'>Py+ccp, Chlorite-Sericite</text><rect x='40' y='310' width='360' height='25' fill='#e0e0e0' rx='3'/><text x='220' y='327' font-family='Arial' font-size='9' text-anchor='middle' fill='#333'>Basement (volcanic)</text></svg>

questions.subject: Domain Knowledge
questions.stem: If porosity decreases from 15% to 10% (constant grain size), permeability decreases by approximately:
questions.solution: k proportional to phi^3/(1-phi)^2. Ratio at 15% vs 10% is ~3.8, so approximately 4x decrease.

figure.markup: <svg xmlns="http://www.w3.org/2000/svg" viewBox="0 0 440 260"><rect width="440" height="260" fill="#fff" rx="8"/><text x="220" y="25" font-family="Arial" font-size="13" font-weight="bold" text-anchor="middle">Kozeny-Carman: k vs porosity</text><rect x="50" y="45" width="350" height="180" fill="#fafafa" rx="4"/><line x1="50" y1="225" x2="400" y2="225" stroke="#333" stroke-width="1"/><line x1="50" y1="45" x2="50" y2="225" stroke="#333" stroke-width="1"/><text x="225" y="250" font-family="Arial" font-size="10" text-anchor="middle">Porosity (phi) %</text><text x="30" y="140" font-family="Arial" font-size="9" text-anchor="middle" transform="rotate(-90,30,140)">Permeability (log)</text><path d="M 80,210 Q 150,180 220,140 Q 290,100 360,60" fill="none" stroke="#1565c0" stroke-width="2"/><circle cx="150" cy="185" r="4" fill="#c62828"/><text x="150" y="200" font-family="Arial" font-size="8" text-anchor="middle" fill="#c62828">15% - k1</text><circle cx="280" cy="110" r="4" fill="#2e7d32"/><text x="280" y="100" font-family="Arial" font-size="8" text-anchor="middle" fill="#2e7d32">10% - k1/4</text><text x="225" y="155" font-family="Arial" font-size="9" text-anchor="middle" fill="#333">Non-linear relationship</text></svg>

questions.subject: Domain Knowledge
questions.stem: Sediment from 5500m depth in the Pacific (CCD ~4500m) is most likely:
questions.solution: Below CCD: calcareous dissolves. Red clay dominates: wind-blown dust, cosmic particles, ~1 mm/kyr accumulation.

questions.subject: Domain Knowledge
questions.stem: The smectite-to-illite transformation releases which ion affecting reservoir quality?
questions.solution: Reaction: Smectite + K+ -> Illite + Quartz + H2O. Released silica precipitates as quartz cement causing porosity loss.

questions.subject: Domain Knowledge
questions.stem: A 25km Plinian eruption column corresponds to a mass discharge rate of approximately:

figure.markup: <svg xmlns="http://www.w3.org/2000/svg" viewBox="0 0 420 280"><rect width="420" height="280" fill="#fff" rx="8"/><text x="210" y="25" font-family="Arial" font-size="13" font-weight="bold" text-anchor="middle">Plinian Eruption Column</text><polygon points="160,250 210,50 260,250" fill="#ffcdd2" stroke="#c62828" stroke-width="2"/><text x="210" y="30" font-family="Arial" font-size="9" text-anchor="middle">25 km</text><text x="280" y="120" font-family="Arial" font-size="9" fill="#333">Convective column</text><text x="280" y="145" font-family="Arial" font-size="9" fill="#333">~1.3e8 kg/s</text><ellipse cx="210" cy="250" rx="80" ry="15" fill="#ffe0b2"/><text x="210" y="254" font-family="Arial" font-size="8" text-anchor="middle">Vent</text><text x="210" y="275" font-family="Arial" font-size="9" text-anchor="middle" fill="#555">VEI 5-6 equivalent</text></svg>

questions.subject: Domain Knowledge
questions.stem: For a horizontal cylinder, depth to axis equals the gravity anomaly half-width. If x_half = 500m, z =
questions.solution: For horizontal cylinder, z = x_half = 500m. Sphere: z=1.3*x_half. Thin dike: z=0.5*x_half.

figure.markup: <svg xmlns="http://www.w3.org/2000/svg" viewBox="0 0 440 260"><rect width="440" height="260" fill="#fff" rx="8"/><text x="220" y="25" font-family="Arial" font-size="13" font-weight="bold" text-anchor="middle">Gravity Half-Width Method</text><rect x="40" y="45" width="360" height="120" fill="#e3f2fd" rx="4"/><text x="220" y="65" font-family="Arial" font-size="10" text-anchor="middle">Bouguer anomaly profile</text><path d="M 80,140 Q 140,100 220,80 Q 300,100 360,140" fill="none" stroke="#1565c0" stroke-width="2"/><line x1="220" y1="80" x2="220" y2="150" stroke="#c62828" stroke-width="1" stroke-dasharray="3,2"/><text x="220" y="75" font-family="Arial" font-size="9" text-anchor="middle" fill="#c62828">max anomaly</text><line x1="220" y1="110" x2="330" y2="110" stroke="#f44336" stroke-width="1.5"/><text x="275" y="105" font-family="Arial" font-size="9" text-anchor="middle" fill="#f44336">x_half = 500m</text><ellipse cx="220" cy="200" rx="30" ry="20" fill="#ffe0b2" stroke="#e65100" stroke-width="2"/><text x="220" y="204" font-family="Arial" font-size="8" text-anchor="middle">Cylinder</text><text x="220" y="245" font-family="Arial" font-size="9" text-anchor="middle" fill="#333">z = x_half for horizontal cylinder</text></svg>

questions.subject: Domain Knowledge
questions.stem: Drag foliation in till (A-axes parallel ice flow, A-B planes dipping upglacier) indicates:
options: Ductile deformation with progressive simple shear
questions.solution: Progressive simple shear. Analogous to S-C fabrics in mylonites.

questions.subject: Domain Knowledge
questions.stem: Cooper-Jacob method: T=2.3Q/(4*pi*ds). With ds=1.5m/cycle and Q=10 L/s, T is approximately:

figure.markup: <svg xmlns="http://www.w3.org/2000/svg" viewBox="0 0 440 260"><rect width="440" height="260" fill="#fff" rx="8"/><text x="220" y="25" font-family="Arial" font-size="13" font-weight="bold" text-anchor="middle">Cooper-Jacob Method</text><rect x="50" y="45" width="350" height="170" fill="#fafafa" rx="4"/><line x1="50" y1="215" x2="400" y2="215" stroke="#333" stroke-width="1"/><line x1="50" y1="45" x2="50" y2="215" stroke="#333" stroke-width="1"/><text x="225" y="240" font-family="Arial" font-size="10" text-anchor="middle">log(t)</text><text x="25" y="135" font-family="Arial" font-size="9" text-anchor="middle" transform="rotate(-90,25,135)">Drawdown s</text><path d="M 80,200 Q 150,180 220,140 L 350,80" fill="none" stroke="#1565c0" stroke-width="2"/><line x1="100" y1="170" x2="350" y2="70" stroke="#f44336" stroke-width="1.5" stroke-dasharray="4,3"/><text x="250" y="60" font-family="Arial" font-size="9" fill="#f44336">Straight line fit</text><text x="220" y="155" font-family="Arial" font-size="9" text-anchor="middle" fill="#333">delta_s per log cycle = slope</text></svg>

questions.subject: Domain Knowledge
questions.stem: The Great Unconformity (Grand Canyon) gap represents approximately:
questions.solution: Separates ~1.75 Ga Vishnu Schist from ~525 Ma Tapeats Sandstone. One of most significant temporal gaps.

questions.subject: Domain Knowledge
questions.stem: Quartz-magnetite delta18O fractionation of 6.0 per mil corresponds to approximately:
questions.solution: Clayton-Epstein geothermometer. For delta=6.0 per mil, T ~ 500C.

questions.subject: Domain Knowledge
questions.stem: In water-wet sandstone, relative permeability to water at residual oil saturation is typically:
questions.solution: At Sor, oil immobile. k_rw typically 0.2-0.4.

figure.markup: <svg xmlns="http://www.w3.org/2000/svg" viewBox="0 0 440 280"><rect width="440" height="280" fill="#fff" rx="8"/><text x="220" y="25" font-family="Arial" font-size="13" font-weight="bold" text-anchor="middle">Relative Permeability Curves</text><rect x="50" y="45" width="350" height="190" fill="#fafafa" rx="4"/><line x1="50" y1="235" x2="400" y2="235" stroke="#333" stroke-width="1"/><line x1="50" y1="45" x2="50" y2="235" stroke="#333" stroke-width="1"/><text x="225" y="260" font-family="Arial" font-size="10" text-anchor="middle">Water saturation (Sw)</text><text x="25" y="145" font-family="Arial" font-size="9" text-anchor="middle" transform="rotate(-90,25,145)">Relative permeability</text><path d="M 80,220 Q 150,200 250,120 Q 320,60 370,50" fill="none" stroke="#1565c0" stroke-width="2"/><text x="380" y="45" font-family="Arial" font-size="8" fill="#1565c0">k_rw</text><path d="M 80,50 Q 150,60 250,150 Q 320,200 370,220" fill="none" stroke="#c62828" stroke-width="2"/><text x="380" y="225" font-family="Arial" font-size="8" fill="#c62828">k_ro</text><line x1="330" y1="50" x2="330" y2="235" stroke="#333" stroke-width="1" stroke-dasharray="3,2"/><text x="330" y="250" font-family="Arial" font-size="8" text-anchor="middle">Sor</text></svg>

questions.subject: Domain Knowledge
questions.stem: Most severe temporal decorrelation in InSAR caused by:
questions.solution: Surface scattering changes between acquisitions. PSInSAR developed to overcome this.

questions.subject: Domain Knowledge
questions.stem: Cu grade in potassic zone relative to phyllic zone in porphyry Cu deposits is typically:
questions.solution: Potassic zone = highest Cu grade, bornite dominant. Also Au-enriched.

questions.subject: Domain Knowledge
questions.stem: If Mg/Ca ratio in foraminifera doubles, the temperature increase is approximately:
questions.solution: Mg/Ca = B*exp(A*T), A~0.09/C. dT = ln(2)/0.09 ~ 7.7C. Closest ~10C.

figure.markup: <svg xmlns="http://www.w3.org/2000/svg" viewBox="0 0 440 260"><rect width="440" height="260" fill="#fff" rx="8"/><text x="220" y="25" font-family="Arial" font-size="13" font-weight="bold" text-anchor="middle">Mg/Ca vs Temperature</text><rect x="50" y="45" width="350" height="170" fill="#fafafa" rx="4"/><line x1="50" y1="215" x2="400" y2="215" stroke="#333" stroke-width="1"/><line x1="50" y1="45" x2="50" y2="215" stroke="#333" stroke-width="1"/><text x="225" y="240" font-family="Arial" font-size="10" text-anchor="middle">Temperature (C)</text><text x="25" y="135" font-family="Arial" font-size="9" text-anchor="middle" transform="rotate(-90,25,135)">Mg/Ca ratio</text><path d="M 80,200 Q 150,180 220,130 Q 290,80 360,50" fill="none" stroke="#1565c0" stroke-width="2"/><circle cx="150" cy="185" r="4" fill="#c62828"/><circle cx="280" cy="100" r="4" fill="#2e7d32"/><text x="215" y="145" font-family="Arial" font-size="9" text-anchor="middle" fill="#333">Exponential relationship</text></svg>

questions.subject: Domain Knowledge
questions.stem: A compound landslide (Varnes 1978) involves:
options: Rotational slump in head + translational slide in toe
questions.solution: Combines rotational slumping (head) with translational sliding (toe).

questions.subject: Domain Knowledge
questions.stem: Diagnostic seismic geometry of a lowstand systems tract:
options: Downlapping onto sequence boundary with progradational clinoforms
questions.solution: LST: progradational geometries with downlapping reflectors onto sequence boundary.

questions.subject: Domain Knowledge
questions.stem: Mars lacks plate tectonics primarily because:
options: Lithosphere too thick/rigid; mantle convection too sluggish
questions.solution: Smaller size -> faster heat loss -> thicker lithosphere (~300-500km). Slower convection.

questions.subject: Domain Knowledge
questions.stem: The Re-Os system (t1/2=41.6 Gyr) is uniquely useful for dating:
options: Upper mantle peridotites and mantle-derived melts
questions.solution: Re and Os are siderophile/chalcophile. Ideal for mantle rocks, sulfide ores, organic-rich shales.

questions.subject: Domain Knowledge
questions.stem: A knickpoint retreats at 1 m/yr over 10 km. Complete retreat takes:
questions.solution: Time = 10000m / 1m/yr = 10000 years.

questions.subject: Domain Knowledge
questions.stem: If a Brazilian disc fails at contact points first, the measured tensile strength:
questions.solution: Contact crushing requires higher load. Overestimates sigma_t.

questions.subject: Domain Knowledge
questions.stem: Peak oil generation occurs at approximately which vitrinite reflectance?
questions.solution: Oil window: Ro 0.6-1.3%, peak ~0.8-0.9%.

figure.markup: <svg xmlns="http://www.w3.org/2000/svg" viewBox="0 0 440 260"><rect width="440" height="260" fill="#fff" rx="8"/><text x="220" y="25" font-family="Arial" font-size="13" font-weight="bold" text-anchor="middle">Oil Window - Vitrinite Reflectance</text><rect x="40" y="45" width="360" height="30" fill="#e8f5e9" rx="3"/><text x="220" y="65" font-family="Arial" font-size="9" text-anchor="middle" fill="#2e7d32">Immature: Ro < 0.5%</text><rect x="40" y="80" width="360" height="40" fill="#c8e6c9" rx="3"/><text x="220" y="105" font-family="Arial" font-size="10" font-weight="bold" text-anchor="middle" fill="#1b5e20">Oil Window: Ro 0.6-1.3%</text><rect x="40" y="125" width="360" height="25" fill="#fff9c4" rx="3"/><text x="220" y="142" font-family="Arial" font-size="9" text-anchor="middle" fill="#f57f17">Peak oil: Ro ~0.8-0.9%</text><rect x="40" y="155" width="360" height="30" fill="#ffe0b2" rx="3"/><text x="220" y="175" font-family="Arial" font-size="9" text-anchor="middle" fill="#e65100">Wet gas: Ro 1.0-1.3%</text><rect x="40" y="190" width="360" height="30" fill="#ffcdd2" rx="3"/><text x="220" y="210" font-family="Arial" font-size="9" text-anchor="middle" fill="#c62828">Dry gas: Ro > 1.3%</text><line x1="40" y1="230" x2="400" y2="230" stroke="#333" stroke-width="1"/><text x="220" y="250" font-family="Arial" font-size="9" text-anchor="middle">Increasing maturity</text></svg>

questions.subject: Domain Knowledge
questions.stem: At fast-spreading ridges, the axial magma chamber is typically:
options: Thin lens (~100m) of 10-50% melt over crystal mush
questions.solution: AMC: thin melt-rich lens at 1-2km depth, underlain by mush zone.

figure.markup: <svg xmlns='http://www.w3.org/2000/svg' viewBox='0 0 480 340'><rect width='480' height='340' fill='#fff' rx='8'/><text x='240' y='25' font-family='Arial' font-size='13' font-weight='bold' text-anchor='middle'>Fast-Spreading Ridge AMC</text><rect x='30' y='40' width='420' height='25' fill='#b3e5fc' rx='3'/><text x='240' y='57' font-family='Arial' font-size='10' text-anchor='middle' fill='#01579b'>Ocean</text><rect x='30' y='70' width='420' height='20' fill='#455a64' rx='3'/><text x='240' y='84' font-family='Arial' font-size='9' text-anchor='middle' fill='#fff'>Basaltic extrusives</text><rect x='30' y='95' width='420' height='30' fill='#5d4037' rx='3'/><text x='240' y='115' font-family='Arial' font-size='9' text-anchor='middle' fill='#fff'>Sheeted dikes</text><rect x='170' y='130' width='140' height='12' fill='#ff8a65' rx='2'/><text x='240' y='139' font-family='Arial' font-size='8' text-anchor='middle' fill='#fff'>Melt lens ~100m</text><rect x='100' y='145' width='280' height='35' fill='#ffccbc' rx='3'/><text x='240' y='168' font-family='Arial' font-size='9' text-anchor='middle' fill='#bf360c'>Crystal mush zone</text><rect x='30' y='185' width='420' height='45' fill='#8d6e63' rx='3'/><text x='240' y='213' font-family='Arial' font-size='9' text-anchor='middle' fill='#fff'>Gabbro cumulates</text><rect x='30' y='235' width='420' height='55' fill='#a1887f' rx='3'/><text x='240' y='268' font-family='Arial' font-size='9' text-anchor='middle' fill='#fff'>Upper mantle (peridotite)</text></svg>

questions.subject: Domain Knowledge
questions.stem: Si4+/O2- radius ratio 0.186 predicts CN=3, yet Si always has CN=4 in silicates because:
options: Pauling rules for metallic bonds only
Si-O bonds have significant covalent character; SiO4 tetrahedron very stable
questions.solution: Si-O bonds have covalent character. SiO4 tetrahedron exceptionally stable.

figure.markup: <svg xmlns="http://www.w3.org/2000/svg" viewBox="0 0 420 260"><rect width="420" height="260" fill="#fff" rx="8"/><text x="210" y="25" font-family="Arial" font-size="13" font-weight="bold" text-anchor="middle">Si-O Tetrahedron</text><polygon points="210,60 150,150 270,150 210,200" fill="none" stroke="#1565c0" stroke-width="2"/><circle cx="210" cy="60" r="12" fill="#ff8a65"/><text x="210" y="64" font-family="Arial" font-size="8" text-anchor="middle" fill="#fff">Si</text><circle cx="150" cy="150" r="10" fill="#90caf9"/><text x="150" y="154" font-family="Arial" font-size="8" text-anchor="middle" fill="#fff">O</text><circle cx="270" cy="150" r="10" fill="#90caf9"/><text x="270" y="154" font-family="Arial" font-size="8" text-anchor="middle" fill="#fff">O</text><circle cx="210" cy="200" r="10" fill="#90caf9"/><text x="210" y="204" font-family="Arial" font-size="8" text-anchor="middle" fill="#fff">O</text><circle cx="210" cy="120" r="10" fill="#90caf9"/><text x="210" y="124" font-family="Arial" font-size="8" text-anchor="middle" fill="#fff">O</text><text x="210" y="245" font-family="Arial" font-size="10" text-anchor="middle" fill="#333">CN=4 despite radius ratio predicting CN=3</text></svg>

questions.subject: Domain Knowledge
questions.stem: The stage between continental rifting and seafloor spreading in the Wilson Cycle:
options: Narrow marine trough with thinned crust, transitional volcanism
questions.solution: Juvenile stage: active rifting, narrow sea. Thinned continental crust, nascent oceanic crust.

questions.subject: Domain Knowledge
questions.stem: Rate-limiting step in pyrite oxidation under unsaturated conditions:
options: O2 diffusion through air-filled pore spaces
questions.solution: O2 diffusion through vadose zone controls rate. D_O2 in air ~1e-5 vs water ~1e-9 m2/s.

questions.subject: Domain Knowledge
questions.stem: Zircon: 206Pb/238U age=730 Ma, 207Pb/235U age=1045 Ma. Discordia through point and origin indicates:
options: Crystallization at 730 Ma with later Pb loss
Crystallization at 730 Ma with Pb gain
questions.solution: Discordia through origin = Pb loss. Upper intercept = crystallization age (>730 Ma).

figure.markup: <svg xmlns='http://www.w3.org/2000/svg' viewBox='0 0 460 360'><rect width='460' height='360' fill='#fff' rx='8'/><text x='230' y='25' font-family='Arial' font-size='13' font-weight='bold' text-anchor='middle'>U-Pb Concordia</text><line x1='50' y1='320' x2='420' y2='320' stroke='#333' stroke-width='1.5'/><line x1='50' y1='40' x2='50' y2='320' stroke='#333' stroke-width='1.5'/><text x='235' y='350' font-family='Arial' font-size='11' text-anchor='middle'>207Pb/235U</text><text x='25' y='180' font-family='Arial' font-size='10' text-anchor='middle' transform='rotate(-90,25,180)'>206Pb/238U</text><path d='M 70,310 Q 180,260 370,70' fill='none' stroke='#1565c0' stroke-width='2'/><text x='130' y='285' font-family='Arial' font-size='9' fill='#1565c0'>500 Ma</text><text x='200' y='230' font-family='Arial' font-size='9' fill='#1565c0'>1000 Ma</text><text x='330' y='100' font-family='Arial' font-size='9' fill='#1565c0'>1500 Ma</text><circle cx='190' cy='240' r='5' fill='#c62828'/><text x='210' y='238' font-family='Arial' font-size='9' fill='#c62828'>Data point</text><line x1='190' y1='240' x2='70' y2='310' stroke='#d32f2f' stroke-width='1.5' stroke-dasharray='4,3'/><text x='120' y='295' font-family='Arial' font-size='9' fill='#d32f2f'>Discordia (Pb loss)</text></svg>

questions.subject: Domain Knowledge
questions.stem: North Atlantic Deep Water is characterized by:
options: Low T, moderate S - cold and salty
questions.solution: NADW: T~2-4C, S~34.9-35.0 PSU. Cold, salty, dense.

figure.markup: <svg xmlns="http://www.w3.org/2000/svg" viewBox="0 0 440 260"><rect width="440" height="260" fill="#fff" rx="8"/><text x="220" y="25" font-family="Arial" font-size="13" font-weight="bold" text-anchor="middle">Thermohaline Circulation</text><rect x="40" y="45" width="360" height="30" fill="#b3e5fc" rx="3"/><text x="220" y="65" font-family="Arial" font-size="9" text-anchor="middle" fill="#01579b">Surface ocean (warm, salty)</text><rect x="40" y="200" width="360" height="30" fill="#1a237e" rx="3"/><text x="220" y="220" font-family="Arial" font-size="9" text-anchor="middle" fill="#fff">Deep ocean (cold, salty)</text><path d="M 120,75 L 120,200" fill="none" stroke="#1565c0" stroke-width="3"/><text x="120" y="140" font-family="Arial" font-size="8" text-anchor="middle" fill="#1565c0">NADW</text><path d="M 320,75 L 320,200" fill="none" stroke="#0d47a1" stroke-width="3"/><text x="320" y="140" font-family="Arial" font-size="8" text-anchor="middle" fill="#0d47a1">AABW</text><text x="80" y="100" font-family="Arial" font-size="8" fill="#333">N Atlantic</text><text x="300" y="100" font-family="Arial" font-size="8" fill="#333">S Ocean</text><text x="220" y="250" font-family="Arial" font-size="9" text-anchor="middle" fill="#555">Density-driven global conveyor belt</text></svg>

questions.subject: Domain Knowledge
questions.stem: Doubly drained clay (H=10m, drainage path=5m), Cv=1e-7 m2/s. Time for 90% consolidation (Tv=0.848):
questions.solution: t = 0.848*25/1e-7 = 2.12e8 s ~ 6.7 years. Closest ~8 years.

questions.subject: Aptitude
questions.stem: If log10(2) = 0.301 and log10(3) = 0.477, the number of digits in 6^20 is:

questions.subject: Aptitude
questions.stem: A shopkeeper marks up goods 40% above cost. Two successive discounts of 10% each result in:
questions.solution: MP=140. After two 10% discounts: 140*0.9*0.9 = 113.4. Profit = 13.4%.

questions.subject: Aptitude
questions.stem: Pipe A fills tank in 12 hrs, B in 15 hrs, C empties in 20 hrs. All open together fill in:

questions.subject: Aptitude
questions.stem: Two dice thrown. Probability sum is a multiple of 3:
questions.solution: Sums 3,6,9,12 are multiples of 3. Favorable = 2+5+4+1 = 12 out of 36 = 1/3.

questions.subject: Aptitude
questions.stem: Mixture milk:water = 7:3. Remove 20% and replace with water. New ratio:
questions.solution: Remaining: 5.6M + 2.4W. Add 2W: 5.6M + 4.4W = 14:11.

questions.subject: Aptitude
questions.stem: A cone melted into cylinder with height = 1/3 of cone's height. Cylinder radius to cone radius ratio:

questions.stem: If x + 1/x = 5, then x^3 + 1/x^3 =

questions.subject: Aptitude
questions.stem: Population increased 10% then decreased 10%. Current = 9900. Two years ago:

questions.subject: Aptitude
questions.stem: If sin(theta) + cos(theta) = sqrt(2), then tan(theta) + cot(theta) =
questions.solution: (sin+cos)^2=2. 1+2sin*cos=2. sin*cos=1/2. tan+cot = 1/(sin*cos) = 2.

questions.subject: Aptitude
questions.stem: How many times do clock hands overlap in 12 hours?
questions.solution: Hands overlap 11 times in 12 hours (approximately every 65.45 minutes).

questions.subject: Aptitude
questions.stem: Average of 50 numbers is 38. If two numbers 45 and 55 are removed, new average:

questions.subject: Aptitude
questions.stem: Rs 5000 at 8% SI for 3 years. Interest earned:

questions.subject: Aptitude
questions.stem: Number of ways to arrange 5 boys and 3 girls in a row so no two girls are together:
questions.solution: Place 5 boys: 5! = 120. 6 gaps choose 3: 6C3 = 20. Arrange girls: 3! = 6. Total = 120*20*6 = 14400.

questions.subject: Aptitude
questions.stem: In a triangle, if each side is doubled, the area increases by:
questions.solution: Area proportional to side^2. Doubling sides gives 4x area. Increase = 4x.

questions.subject: Aptitude
questions.stem: Two vessels contain milk:water = 3:2 and 5:3. Ratio to mix for 1:1:
questions.solution: Vessel1: 3/5 milk. Vessel2: 5/8 milk. For 1/2 milk: (3/5)x + (5/8)(1-x) = 1/2. x = 5/13.

questions.subject: Aptitude
questions.stem: Two pipes fill a tank in 10 and 15 hours. A leak empties in 30 hours. All open:

questions.subject: Aptitude
questions.stem: Simplify: (sqrt(5)+sqrt(3))/(sqrt(5)-sqrt(3)):
questions.solution: Multiply by conjugate: (5+3+2sqrt(15))/(5-3) = (8+2sqrt(15))/2 = 4+sqrt(15).

questions.subject: Aptitude
questions.stem: LCM of two numbers is 2520, HCF is 4. If one number is 84, other:

questions.subject: Aptitude
questions.stem: Father is 3 times son's age. In 10 years, father will be twice son's age. Present ages:

questions.stem: Boat speed 10 km/h, stream speed 2 km/h. Time to go 24 km upstream and return:

questions.subject: Aptitude
questions.stem: A and B invest 60000 and 80000. Profit 28000. A's share:

questions.subject: Aptitude
questions.stem: A rectangular field length increased 20%, breadth reduced 10%. Change in area:
questions.solution: New area = 1.2*0.9 = 1.08. Increase = 8%.

questions.subject: Aptitude
questions.stem: HCF of 12, 18, and 24 is:

questions.subject: Aptitude
questions.stem: Rs 2000 at 10% CI for 2 years compounded annually:

questions.subject: General Awareness
questions.stem: Which river is known as the 'Sorrow of Bihar'?
questions.solution: Kosi river is known as Sorrow of Bihar due to devastating floods from shifting course.

questions.subject: General Awareness
questions.stem: Which Five Year Plan adopted the objective of 'growth with social justice and equity'?
questions.solution: Ninth Five Year Plan (1997-2002) focused on growth with social justice and equity.

questions.subject: General Awareness
questions.stem: Article 370 of the Indian Constitution was related to:
questions.solution: Article 370 granted special autonomous status to Jammu & Kashmir. It was abrogated in August 2019.

questions.subject: General Awareness
questions.stem: The Chandrayaan-3 mission successfully landed on the Moon's south pole region in:
questions.solution: Chandrayaan-3 Vikram lander successfully landed on August 23, 2023 near the Moon's south pole.

questions.subject: General Awareness
questions.stem: India's first underwater metro tunnel is located in:
questions.solution: Kolkata's East-West Metro has India's first underwater metro tunnel under the Hooghly River.

questions.subject: General Awareness
questions.stem: The Battle of Plassey was fought in:
questions.solution: Battle of Plassey was fought in 1767 between Siraj-ud-Daulah and the British East India Company.

questions.subject: General Awareness
questions.stem: Which among the following is NOT a greenhouse gas?
questions.solution: Oxygen (O2) is not a greenhouse gas. CO2, CH4, N2O, and water vapour are greenhouse gases.

questions.subject: General Awareness
questions.stem: The Tropic of Cancer passes through how many Indian states?
questions.solution: Tropic of Cancer passes through 8 Indian states: Gujarat, Rajasthan, MP, Chhattisgarh, Jharkhand, WB, Tripura, Mizoram.

questions.subject: General Awareness
questions.stem: Bharat Ratna 2024 was awarded to:
options: Karpoori Thakur, PV Narasimha Rao, MS Swaminathan, LK Advani
questions.solution: In 2024, Bharat Ratna was awarded to Karpoori Thakur, PV Narasimha Rao, MS Swaminathan, and LK Advani.

questions.subject: General Awareness
questions.stem: The 73rd Constitutional Amendment Act is related to:
questions.solution: 73rd Amendment (1992) constitutionalized Panchayati Raj Institutions in India.

questions.subject: English Language
questions.stem: Choose the synonym of 'Ephemeral':
questions.solution: Ephemeral means short-lived, fleeting, transient. Opposite of permanent.

questions.subject: English Language
questions.stem: Choose the antonym of 'Pragmatic':
questions.solution: Pragmatic = practical, realistic. Idealistic is the antonym.

questions.subject: English Language
questions.stem: What does 'to burn the midnight oil' mean?
options: To work late into the night
questions.solution: Burn the midnight oil = to work/study late into the night.

questions.subject: English Language
questions.stem: Choose correct: 'By the time we arrived, the train ______.'
questions.solution: Past perfect 'had left' for action completed before another past action.

questions.subject: English Language
questions.stem: Choose correct: 'He is proficient ______ English.'
questions.solution: Proficient IN a language is the correct collocation.

questions.subject: English Language
questions.stem: In the sentence 'The geologist _elucidated_ the complex formation', the word elucidated means:
questions.solution: Elucidated = made clear, explained in detail.

questions.subject: English Language
questions.stem: Convert to passive: 'The team discovered a new mineral deposit.'
options: A new mineral deposit was discovered by the team.
A new mineral deposit is discovered by the team.
A new mineral deposit has been discovered by the team.
A new mineral deposit were discovered by the team.
questions.solution: Active: team discovered -> Passive: was discovered by the team.

questions.subject: English Language
questions.stem: Choose correct: 'She is ______ honest person.'
questions.solution: 'An' before vowel sounds. 'Honest' starts with silent h, vowel sound.

questions.subject: English Language
questions.stem: 'One who loves books' is called:
questions.solution: Bibliophile = book lover. Philatelist = stamp collector. Numismatist = coin collector.

questions.subject: English Language
questions.stem: Choose correct: '______ it rained heavily, the match continued.'
questions.solution: Although = contrast connector. Despite heavy rain, match continued.
